q:
What has a face and two hands
but no arms or legs?
What does a house wear?
Where would you take a sick boat?

I give milk and I have a horn,
but I'm not a cow.
What am I?

Why did the fly never land
on the computer?
a:
He was afraid of the
world wide web.

q:
What creature is smarter
than a talking parrot?

What rock group has four famous men,
but none of them sing?

What kind of murderer
is full of fiber?

I have hundreds of wheels,
but move I do not.
Call me what I am;
call me a lot. What am I?
How does a bee get to school?

What kind of running
leads to walking?

I start with M, end with X,
and have a never-ending
amount of letters. What am I?
Why are A's like flowers?

What is orange and
sounds like a parrot?

A farmer has 20 sheep, 10 pigs,
and 10 cows. If we call the pigs
cows, how many cows does he have?
a:
Ten. Calling pigs cows
doesn't make them cows.

q: What do you call a snail on a ship?

I make a loud sound when I change.
When I do, I get bigger
but weigh less. What am I?
Why did the skeleton skip the party?
a: He had no body to go with.

q:
I travel all around the world
but never leave the corner.
What am I?

What can go up a chimney down,
but can't go down a chimney up?

How do you spell COW
in thirteen letters?
Why is an island like the letter T?
a: Both are in the middle of water!

q:
What has wheels and flies,
but is not an aircraft?
What tastes better than it smells?

What do you call a bear
without ears?

What do you get if you cross
poison ivy with a four-leaf clover?
Why is Europe like a frying pan?
a: It has Greece at the bottom.

q:
What do you call a fly
without wings?

What word has five letters
but sounds like it only has one?

What is 3/7 chicken, 2/3 cat,
and 2/4 goat?

A pet shop owner said his parrot
repeats everything it hears.
A buyer returned it two weeks later —
it hadn't said a word.
The owner hadn't lied. How?

Which part of a road do
ghosts love most?

Why can't a pirate
ever finish the alphabet?
a: He always gets lost at C!

q:
What do Alexander the Great
and Winnie the Pooh
have in common?
